rows.description: Verify prerequisites (Node 22+, pnpm, git, just, Playwright deps, Anthropic SDK auth) before starting Phase 0 harness build.
steps: Confirm `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` OR Claude Max session OR Bedrock/Vertex env present
Produce evidence/phase0-step-001-result.txt with each tool version
Flip passes:true after evidence is observed
rows.approach: Pure verification row. Mirrors agentmark scaffolding 00-prerequisites.md. No code; gates the rest of Phase 0 so missing tools fail fast.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip preflight; let downstream rows fail
rejected_alternatives.reason: A missing Playwright binary mid Phase-3 wiring would re-litigate ~30 verifier rows. Cheap to catch on day one.

expected_outcomes: evidence file lists 5 tool versions with go/no-go verdict
Any missing tool blocks Phase 0 entry

rows.description: Create factory folder skeleton: .claude/{agents,skills,hooks,rules,templates,state}/, schemas/, scaffolding/, packages/, scripts/, evidence/, contracts/, contexts/, investigations/, reports/, projects/.
steps: Mkdir -p the full tree per agentmark scaffolding 01-folder-skeleton.md
rows.approach: Establish the directory contract first so every downstream row writes to a known place. Empty .gitkeep'd dirs are fine.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Lazy-create dirs as rows need them
rejected_alternatives.reason: Lazy creation hides the contract. Every Phase 0 reviewer should be able to `tree .claude/` on day one and see the intended shape.

rows.description: Author the 6 root artifacts: CLAUDE.md (startup ritual + hard rules), PROGRESS.md (append-only handoff), phase-plan.md (living rebuild manifest), feature_list.json (this file), LESSONS.md, DECISIONS.md (ADR log).
steps: Write CLAUDE.md with startup ritual section + hard rules referencing protected-files-policy + testing-policy
Write PROGRESS.md with template heading `## YYYY-MM-DD — <topic>`
Write phase-plan.md with the living-rebuild-manifest preamble (mirrors agentmark phase-plan.md)
Write LESSONS.md skeleton + DECISIONS.md with ADR-000 ("adopt harness scaffolding pattern")
rows.approach: Mirror agentmark scaffolding 02-root-artifacts.md. These 6 files form the canonical context every fresh session loads. They're inert until later rows wire skills against them.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Generate root artifacts from a template at /new-project time only
rejected_alternatives.reason: These artifacts govern the FACTORY itself, not generated projects. They must exist at factory root from Phase 0 so /sync-phase-plan and /capture-lesson have somewhere to write.

expected_outcomes: All 6 files exist with non-trivial content
phase-plan.md opens with the living-manifest header

rows.description: Wire .claude/settings.json with PreToolUse hooks (block-dangerous, enforce-boundaries, validate-brief, detect-loop), PostToolUse prettier, SessionStart load-context reminder, and allow/deny permission lists.
steps: Author .claude/settings.json mirroring Phase 2's wiring (block-dangerous Bash, enforce-boundaries on Write/Edit/MultiEdit, detect-loop on *, prettier PostToolUse on Write/Edit)
Allow list: Read, Grep, Glob, just, git status/diff/log/branch/show/rev-parse, ls, pnpm run/test/typecheck/lint, docker compose/ps/logs/exec/inspect
Deny list: rm, curl|, wget, git push --force (no --force-with-lease deny), docker rm/rmi/volume rm/prune, npm/pnpm/yarn/bun publish
rows.approach: Phase 2 ships a battle-tested settings.json; port it verbatim and adjust only the hook paths. Hooks themselves are authored in subsequent rows.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Start with empty settings.json and add hooks one at a time
rejected_alternatives.reason: Hook wiring is declarative; one row covers the whole config. Splitting it spreads context across 4 rows for no benefit.

expected_outcomes: All 4 hook entries reference .claude/hooks/<name>.{mjs,sh} paths that exist (or are created in next rows)

rows.description: Author .claude/hooks/block-dangerous.sh — PreToolUse Bash guard blocking rm -rf /, force-push to main, SQL drop/truncate, npm publish, deploy cmds, Tier B data-wipe (prisma reset, drizzle drop, S3 sync --delete). Exempts --force-with-lease and --dry-run.
steps: Verify `./block-dangerous.sh <<< 'git push --force-with-lease origin feat/x'` exits zero
rows.approach: Fail-closed safety net. Phase 2's implementation is proven; port verbatim.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Rewrite in mjs for cross-platform consistency
rejected_alternatives.reason: Bash-only is fine: this hook only fires on Bash tool calls. Rewriting risks regression in a load-bearing safety hook.

rows.description: Author .claude/hooks/enforce-boundaries.sh — PreToolUse Write/Edit guard blocking writes outside $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (with harness-state exception ~/.claude/projects/{slug}/) and blocking sensitive files (.env, *.pem, *.key, SSH keys, credentials.json, code-signing certs, .keystore).
steps: Self-test: write to /tmp/foo.txt outside project → deny
rows.approach: Load-bearing scope guard. Cascades to git-agent for cross-boundary edits; everything else is denied.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Use Claude Code's built-in permission prompts only
rejected_alternatives.reason: Permission prompts are user-facing; this hook is policy-level and silent for the model. Both defenses stack.

rows.description: Author .claude/hooks/detect-loop.mjs — PreToolUse circuit breaker blocking the 3rd identical action in a row (hash of tool + file/cmd + content + discriminators). Carve-out for Playwright capture tools. Rolling state in recent-attempts.json (50 entries).
steps: Self-test: 3 identical Bash calls in a row → third denied
rows.approach: Circuit breaker against blind retries burning bug-fixer attempts 3-5. Fail-open on unparseable input so model isn't blocked by hook bugs.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Too aggressive — many legit workflows touch the same file twice in a row (Edit then re-Edit after read).

expected_outcomes: Playwright carve-out lets capture loops through

rows.description: Author .claude/hooks/verify-gate.mjs — PreToolUse gate on Edit/Write/MultiEdit targeting feature_list.json. Blocks passes:false → passes:true flips unless the row's evidence file has been read this session (read-log in .claude/state/evidence-reads.json).
steps: Implement verify-gate.mjs: parse Edit/Write diff, find {id, passes:false→true} delta, check evidence path in read-log
Self-test: flip phase0-step-001 to passes:true without reading evidence/phase0-step-001-result.txt → deny
Self-test: read the file first, then flip → allow
rows.approach: Core anti-hallucination gate from agentmark scaffolding 04. Prevents model from marking work done without observing artifacts.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Trust the model to only flip rows after reading evidence
rejected_alternatives.reason: Phase 2's 200+ bugs included multiple instances of the model claiming work done without verification. The gate is cheap; the bug surface is huge.

expected_outcomes: Both self-test probes return expected verdicts

rows.description: Author .claude/hooks/track-read.mjs — PostToolUse on Read tool. Appends {tool:'Read', file_path, timestamp} to .claude/state/evidence-reads.json so verify-gate.mjs can consult it.
steps: Implement track-read.mjs: read PostToolUse payload, append to evidence-reads.json with file-locking
Self-test: Read evidence/phase0-step-001-result.txt, then assert entry appended
rows.approach: Pair to verify-gate.mjs. Together they form the evidence-before-flip invariant.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Tail the SDK transcript for Read events instead of hooking
rejected_alternatives.reason: Hooks fire reliably per-event; transcript parsing adds a dependency on log format that can drift.

expected_outcomes: Entry appears in evidence-reads.json after Read
Concurrent writes don't corrupt the log

rows.description: Author .claude/hooks/commit-on-stop.mjs — Stop-event auto-checkpoint. On session stop, if there are uncommitted changes under tracked paths, `git add -A && git commit -m "checkpoint: <session-id> <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM>"`.
steps: Implement commit-on-stop.mjs: detect uncommitted changes via `git status --porcelain`
Skip if working tree clean OR if commit would include files in .gitignore
Use HEREDOC for commit message; never --no-verify
Self-test: leave a dirty file, simulate Stop event, assert commit created
rows.approach: Backstop against session-end work loss. Phase 2 lost a partial bug-134 rename to a missed commit — this prevents recurrence.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip auto-commit; rely on operator discipline
rejected_alternatives.reason: Operator forgets on long debug sessions. A checkpoint commit is reversible; lost work isn't.

expected_outcomes: Dirty tree → commit created with checkpoint message

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/evaluator.md — skeptical PASS/NEEDS_WORK reviewer subagent (Sonnet). Reads a row from feature_list.json + its evidence file + the diff; returns structured verdict. Includes plan-parity rubric per agentmark scaffolding 07.
steps: Author agent file with frontmatter (model: sonnet, tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash) — body prompt left for execution
Define output schema in .claude/agents/evaluator.schema.json (verdict: PASS|NEEDS_WORK, reasons[], plan_parity_drift_pct)
rows.approach: Fresh-context reviewer eliminates the same-session bias that lets work-in-progress feel finished. Sonnet model balances cost and judgment quality.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Cost overkill for a verdict task. Sonnet 4.6 passes the rubric reliably in agentmark.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Inline review in main agent context
rejected_alternatives.reason: Defeats the purpose — main agent has authored the work; can't be skeptical about it.

expected_outcomes: Schema validates a sample verdict object

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/retro.md — phase-gate retrospective subagent (Sonnet). Samples 5 random rows from feature_list.json, audits drift between row outcome_summary and actual code; flags >20% drift as blocker.
steps: Author agent file with frontmatter (model: sonnet, tools: Read, Grep, Glob)
Define output schema (rows_sampled[], drift_pct, blockers[], recommendations[])
rows.approach: Closes the rebuild guarantee loop. At each phase boundary the retro samples random rows and verifies the plan still describes shipped reality.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Audit every row at phase gate
rejected_alternatives.reason: Too slow on 180-row phases. Random sampling is statistically sound and finishes in <5 min.

expected_outcomes: Sample run on a known-drifted row catches the drift

rows.description: Port harness workflow skills from agentmark scaffolding: check-existing-work, plan-bug, plan-feature, plan-investigation, capture-lesson, consult-lessons. Each is a .claude/skills/<slug>/SKILL.md.
steps: Verify each has YAML frontmatter (name, description, allowed-tools) that parses
Smoke each: invoke once, observe expected output
rows.approach: Batched port. These 6 skills are stable in agentmark; no rewrites needed. Each one's SKILL.md body is short and self-contained.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Each skill is ~50 lines of port; combined they're still under one Opus session.

expected_outcomes: All 6 skill files present with valid frontmatter
/check-existing-work returns a non-empty report on this repo

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/sync-phase-plan/SKILL.md — folds shipped reality into phase-plan.md. Detects durable behavior in code that's missing from the plan; proposes diff for operator confirmation.
steps: Self-test: deliberately drift one phase-plan §, run /sync-phase-plan, observe correction proposed
rows.approach: The rebuild guarantee skill. Without /sync-phase-plan the plan rots and re-build produces a different system than what's shipped.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Operator should confirm plan deltas — they sometimes encode intent the code doesn't show.

expected_outcomes: Operator confirmation step exists before write

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/{save-context,load-context-chain,phase-gate,polish-pass}/SKILL.md — context-snapshot pair + phase-gate retro invoker + polish-pass flipper. Ported from agentmark.
steps: Each: verify frontmatter parses + smoke once
save-context writes to contexts/<YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM>-<slug>.md; load-context-chain walks backward
phase-gate invokes retro agent (row 012); polish-pass flips polished:false→true after bench script passes
rows.approach: Batched port of 4 small context/lifecycle skills. Each is operator-invoked.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Defer polish-pass until first real polish need
rejected_alternatives.reason: polished:false rows accumulate fast; better to have the flipper ready than retrofit it.

rows.description: HUMAN: exercise the harness baseline end-to-end with a throwaway feature; confirm verify-gate denies a flip without evidence; confirm /capture-lesson appends; confirm evaluator returns PASS or NEEDS_WORK.
steps: Add a throwaway row phase0-step-099 to feature_list.json
Attempt to flip it without reading its evidence file — confirm verify-gate denies
Read the evidence file, then flip — confirm allowed
Invoke /capture-lesson with a sample lesson; confirm LESSONS.md grows
Dispatch evaluator agent on the throwaway row; confirm verdict
rows.approach: Mirrors agentmark scaffolding 18-smoke-test.md. Single end-to-end exercise proves the harness composes correctly before Phase 0 continues.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip smoke test; trust each individual row's self-test
rejected_alternatives.reason: Composition bugs (hook fires twice, evaluator can't read agent file, etc.) only surface end-to-end.

expected_outcomes: Operator signs off in evidence file

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/auth-provider.ts — resolves Anthropic / Claude Max / Bedrock / Vertex into SDK options. Pure resolver; mutates env copy, never process.env.
steps: Unit test: each of 4 providers returns expected SDK options shape
Unit test: process.env unmodified after resolveAuthOptions()
rows.approach: Foundational dispatch infra. Every invoke-agent call routes through this. Phase 2's impl is solid; port verbatim.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Side effects pollute parallel dispatches. Pure resolver returns options per call.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/model-config.ts + .claude/models.yaml + ~/.claude/models.yaml merge. Resolves per-agent settings (model, effort, budget, stallTimeoutMs). Per-agent overrides + tier defaults.
steps: Author .claude/models.yaml with defaults for each subagent (builder=sonnet, evaluator=sonnet, perceptual-reviewer=sonnet, others=opus per agentmark norms)
rows.approach: Single source of truth for model selection. User-home override lets developers swap models without editing the factory.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Hardcode model per agent in agent frontmatter
rejected_alternatives.reason: Frontmatter is per-agent; budget table needs cross-agent rollup. yaml lets one operator tweak globally.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/budget-tracker.ts — pipeline-wide cost accumulator with per-model breakdowns (Sonnet/Opus/Haiku), pipeline cap + per-stage caps, BudgetExceededError sentinel.
steps: Wire into invoke-agent (row 022) post-return for spend recording
Unit test: per-stage cap fires BudgetExceededError
rows.approach: Cost-control backstop. Without this a single runaway agent could burn a month's budget. Phase 2's table-driven caps are proven.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Dashboard is reactive (minutes-late); per-stage cap is proactive (next call blocked).

expected_outcomes: Per-model rollup matches sum of dispatches

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/retry-counters.ts + state-persistence.ts — 5-tier retry budget (layer5=3, visual-review=3, task-retry=2, merge-conflict=3, kit-change-request=2) + snapshot/restore to .claude/state/{pipelineRunId}/counters.json (atomic).
steps: Unit test: counter increment + cap reached
rows.approach: Crash-recovery enabler. Pipeline runs span hours; without persisted counters a mid-run crash forgets what's already been retried.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Loses state on crash; orchestrator restarts can over-retry past caps.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/pause.ts + agent-mcp-config.ts — PauseSignal sentinel + atomic paused.json write; agent frontmatter mcp_servers[] parser that filters factory .mcp.json per dispatch.
steps: Port Phase 2 pause.ts: pauseRun() writes paused.json atomically, throws PauseSignal
Port agent-mcp-config.ts: parse frontmatter `mcp_servers: [...]`, filter .mcp.json, return per-dispatch subset
Unit test: pauseRun() round-trip + sentinel propagation
Unit test: agent without mcp_servers returns full .mcp.json (back-compat)
rows.approach: Pause is a Mode B requirement; MCP filter is a per-dispatch performance + safety win. Both are small modules; one row covers them.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Always pass full .mcp.json to every dispatch
rejected_alternatives.reason: Each MCP server adds tool surface area; over-provisioned tools dilute model focus and burn tokens.

expected_outcomes: MCP filter returns expected subset for known agent frontmatter

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/invoke-agent.ts — SDK dispatch wrapper. Builds agent prompt, injects bug-fix context, reads agent output JSON, validates vs schema, surfaces violations/regressions, handles tester-diff audit, worktree seed/commit/install.
steps: Port Phase 2 invoke-agent.ts (resolve the in-flight bug-134 transition; pick the final implementation, drop the .bug-134-final companion)
Unit test: agent returns valid output → caller receives parsed object; invalid output → schema error surfaced
rows.approach: The single dispatch chokepoint. Every agent call flows through here. Resolving the bug-134 ambiguity in Phase 2 is part of the port — pick one impl and commit.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Keep the .bug-134-final companion as a fallback
rejected_alternatives.reason: Two-file ambiguity is a maintenance trap. Pick the canonical impl; deleting the companion is reversible via git.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip schema validation; trust agent output
rejected_alternatives.reason: Phase 2 agents hallucinated output shapes multiple times; validation catches it before downstream consumers crash.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/cli.ts + cli-runner.ts — Commander entry with flags (--resume-from-stage, --dry-run, --require-pr-review, --max-concurrent, --pipeline-run-id, --bugs-yaml-mode) + cli-runner orchestrating Mode A → Mode B → rounds.
steps: Port Phase 2 cli.ts + cli-runner.ts; finish the unfinished orchestration seams (pause/resume handoff between modes)
Unit test: each flag parses; --dry-run short-circuits each mode
rows.approach: Operator entry point. Phase 2 left seams between Mode A→B→rounds; resolve them by passing pipelineRunId + state directory through cleanly.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Ship cli with --resume disabled until Phase 2-rebuild rows are done
rejected_alternatives.reason: Resume is the single most-used flag in long pipelines; ship it day one.

expected_outcomes: Dry-run short-circuit works for each mode

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/protected-files.ts — hard guard with 4 classes (PROTECTED_FILES absolute paths, PROTECTED_PACKAGES_FILES glob, PROTECTED_CONTENT_INVARIANTS substrings, first-match tuples). Called post-dispatch; rolls back merge on violation.
steps: Port Phase 2 protected-files.ts (the canonical machine-readable manifest)
Unit test: each of 4 classes catches its violation
Unit test: rollback path restores worktree state
rows.approach: Bug-091 hardening. Phase 2's reading-log-02 incident (deleted postcss.config.mjs) drove this guard. It's the single most-load-bearing post-dispatch check.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Phase 2 tried soft-only first; agents deleted protected files anyway. Hard rollback is the only reliable guard.

rows.description: Author .claude/rules/{protected-files-policy.md,testing-policy.md} — port Phase 2 rule docs. Protected-files: 4 classes + canonical manifest reference. Testing-policy: hybrid TDD (builder 60% / tester 80%), 3 hard constraints, 3 seeding strategies, 6 anti-patterns.
steps: Cross-reference: protected-files-policy.md cites orchestrator/src/protected-files.ts as canonical manifest
Cross-reference: testing-policy.md cites tester-diff-audit.ts (row authored in Phase 3) for anti-pattern enforcement
rows.approach: Soft enforcement layer. Hard enforcement lives in code (protected-files.ts, tester-diff-audit.ts); rules give agents the rationale they need to follow soft norms.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip rule docs; let code enforcement speak for itself
rejected_alternatives.reason: Hard rollback teaches what's blocked, not why. Rules give agents the model to avoid violations in the first place.

expected_outcomes: Cross-refs to canonical-manifest paths are valid

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/new-project/SKILL.md — scaffold projects/<slug>/ by cloning factory resources (.claude/agents, .claude/skills, .claude/hooks, .claude/rules, schemas/, templates/). Flags: --proposal (auto-fills brief via /draft-brief), --agentic-visibility (public/private/split for git-tracking).
steps: Implement --agentic-visibility flag: public (.claude tracked), private (gitignored), split (project code tracked, .claude gitignored)
Smoke: /new-project test-app --proposal "a todo app" creates projects/test-app/ with brief.md drafted
rows.approach: The factory's primary externally-visible operation. Clone-pattern is the single mechanism by which a project gets its agentic capability; integrity here matters more than convenience.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Symlinks break per-project customization (a project may patch a skill); cloning gives each project its own copy to evolve.

rows.description: HUMAN: run /new-project on a sample brand; inspect projects/<slug>/ tree, brief.md draft, and gitignore partitioning. Confirm clone integrity (no missing files, no extra files).
steps: Diff projects/sample-brand/.claude/ vs factory .claude/ (should be byte-identical except for state/ which is empty in project)
Read projects/sample-brand/brief.md; confirm 20 sections present with NEEDS_CLARIFICATION markers where the proposal was silent
Verify git status: project code tracked, .claude/ ignored per visibility setting
rows.approach: First interactive boundary in the operator journey. Inspection catches clone bugs (file permissions, missing files, unintended state pollution) before downstream skills depend on the project tree.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip human inspection; rely on row 026 self-tests
rejected_alternatives.reason: Self-tests verify the happy path; human inspection catches surprises like accidentally-cloned credentials or state from the factory.

expected_outcomes: Operator signs off on clone integrity
Any anomalies filed as phase0-step-027-followup rows

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/{draft-brief,validate-brief,scan-assets,delete-project,check-existing-work}/SKILL.md — project-lifecycle skills. Ported batch from Phase 2.
steps: draft-brief: accepts --proposal "<text>" OR file path; fills 20 sections with HTML-comment NEEDS_CLARIFICATION markers
scan-assets: walks assets/, emits docs/asset-inventory.json + assets/INVENTORY.md
check-existing-work: pre-flight audit (architecture.yaml version, tasks.yaml status, feature branches, worktree state)
rows.approach: Batched port of 5 small project-lifecycle skills. Each is operator-invoked and has clear input/output contracts from Phase 2.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Each is a verbatim port; sum is under one Opus session.

expected_outcomes: All 5 SKILL.md files present with parsing frontmatter
Each smokes against the test-app from row 026

rows.description: Author .claude/hooks/validate-brief.mjs — PreToolUse Write/Edit/MultiEdit guard on brief.md. Frontmatter YAML schema check + §7 Architecture / §10 Navigation code-block presence. In-memory simulation; deny via hookSpecificOutput.
steps: Self-test: write brief with malformed frontmatter → deny
Self-test: write brief missing §7 code block → deny
rows.approach: Prevents bad brief edits from committing. Pairs with /validate-brief skill (row 028) — hook catches inline edits, skill validates on demand.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Skill is operator-invoked; hook fires automatically on every edit. Both layers stack.

expected_outcomes: In-memory simulation doesn't write to disk

rows.description: Author 16 JSON schemas under schemas/ (brief-frontmatter, brief-capabilities, navigation, architecture, tasks, tasks-coverage, feature, feature-context, screens, screen-fixture, user-flows-manifest, bugs-yaml, build-to-spec-verify-output, parity-verify-output, visual-review-report, signoff). Ported from Phase 2.
steps: Port all 16 schema .json files verbatim from Phase 2 schemas/
Verify each parses as valid JSON Schema (draft-07 or later)
Unit test each against a known-good + known-bad fixture
Produce evidence/phase0-step-030-result.txt with per-schema validation result
rows.approach: Schemas are the contract layer between orchestrator and agents. Phase 2's set is comprehensive; port verbatim.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Lazy-author schemas as orchestrator modules need them
rejected_alternatives.reason: Lazy authoring lets schema drift creep in. One-shot port gives the consumer-side a known surface.

expected_outcomes: Each has at least one known-good + known-bad probe passing

rows.description: Author packages/orchestrator-contracts (Zod schemas + TS types) — common.ts (RoundState, StageSchemas lookup), stages.ts (21 stage definitions), tasks.ts (Task, WorkItem), feature-context.ts, git-agent.ts, model-config.ts. 121 tests.
steps: Verify Zod schemas align with JSON schemas (row 030) for shared types
Run package's test suite (121 tests expected); all pass
rows.approach: Zod gives TS-side type safety; the JSON schemas give cross-language validation. Both layers compile from the same conceptual contracts.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Schemas are language-agnostic; cross-tool validation (e.g. agent prompts referencing schema paths) needs the JSON form.

rows.description: Author .claude/templates/ui-kit-{contract.md,eslint-plugin/,tsconfig-consumer.json,validate-consumer.ts} — kit consumption rules (6 + escapes + enforcement), 4 ESLint rules (no-arbitrary-tailwind, no-deep-imports, no-hex-in-className, no-inline-style-tokens), barrel-only path alias, validator stub.
steps: Verify ESLint plugin loads in a sample consumer (apps/web stub)
Verify each rule fires on its known-bad fixture
rows.approach: Templates ship with /new-project; ui-kit contract enforcement is what stops builders from regressing the design system.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Reviewer fires late; ESLint fires on every save. Catching violations early saves builder iterations.

expected_outcomes: All 4 rules fire on bad fixtures

rows.description: Author .claude/templates/ HTML scaffolds (mockups-index-template.html, user-flows-template.html, worktrees-README.md) + 4 Dockerfile templates (node-express, node-fastify, node-trpc-nest, python-fastapi) + 4 dev-multi-tier templates + 4 E2E seeding templates (playwright-global-setup, seed-intercept page.route, seed-localstorage, seed-db /test/seed contract).
steps: Port all template files verbatim from Phase 2
Verify each Dockerfile builds against a minimal sample project (one per stack)
Verify each dev-multi-tier template boots without crash
Verify each E2E seeding template type-checks in a Playwright project
rows.approach: Static templates with no logic; smoke tests are the only verification needed. Bundled into one row since each is ~50-150 lines of port.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip Docker templates until a project actually deploys
rejected_alternatives.reason: Architect references these by name; their absence blocks /architect output validation.

rows.description: Author .claude/architecture.yaml.template + .claude/models.yaml.template — schema-shaped placeholders that /architect (Phase 1) and model-config (Phase 0) read.
steps: Port Phase 2 architecture.yaml.template (all sections: apps, persistence, stack, compliance, vendors, env-vars)
Author models.yaml.template with per-agent default model + tier + budget
rows.approach: Templates make /architect's job mechanical: fill placeholders with reasoned choices. Schema cross-check catches template drift.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Schema doesn't encode operator-friendly comments and section ordering; hand-authored template carries that affordance.

expected_outcomes: models.yaml.template has entry for every agent in row 030's list

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/analyst.md — orchestrates 5 parallel sub-workers via Agent tool: A) competitive research (WebSearch/Fetch), B) voice/archetype synthesis, C) visual signature, D) per-direction mockup rendering, E) shared analysis (styles, assets, inspirations). Mode A.
steps: Author agent file with frontmatter (model, allowed-tools, mcp_servers) per row 030/031 schemas
Body prompt is left for execution time per the rebuild prompt constraint
Sub-worker prompts are INLINE in the agent body (per Phase 2 LESSONS phase1-step-031..040 pattern)
Smoke: /analyze 3 dispatches and observes 5 parallel sub-workers
rows.approach: Pattern: worker fan-out via Task-tool calls in a single message. Phase 2 proves this scales to 5 workers; same approach here.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Coordination overhead; inline sub-workers share the analyst's context window and let the synthesis worker reason across siblings.

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/{architect,project-manager,ui-designer,skills-agent}.md — Mode A subagents. Architect: architecture.yaml + .env.example + credentials checklist (hash-detect re-run). PM: --mode=tasks (tasks.yaml v2) + --mode=kit-change-request (detour). UI Designer: mockups + ui-kit + screens, vision-capable. Skills-agent: --scope=design|build, idempotent.
steps: Author 4 agent files with frontmatter (model, tools, mcp_servers per architect.yaml.template-defined design vs build scope)
ui-designer: do NOT hardcode MCP scope in frontmatter (resolve Phase 2's [UNFINISHED] item via dynamic resolution at dispatch time, see row 022 invoke-agent + row 021 agent-mcp-config)
Smoke each agent against its expected input fixture
rows.approach: Batched. Each agent is a frontmatter + tool/MCP wiring concern; bodies authored at execution time.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Author each agent in its own row
rejected_alternatives.reason: Frontmatter + tool wiring is mechanical; 4 in one row stays under session size.

expected_outcomes: ui-designer MCP scope resolves dynamically (no hardcoded list)

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/{web-frontend-builder,mobile-frontend-builder,backend-builder}.md — stack-agnostic builders. Each reads architecture.yaml.tooling.stack.<scope>, dispatches to .claude/skills/agents/<scope>/{slug}/. Generates code + sibling happy-path tests. Mode B, hybrid TDD 60%.
steps: Frontmatter declares per-builder allowed-tools (Write/Edit/MultiEdit, Read, Bash for pnpm/uv, Grep, Glob)
Each smokes against a test-app stub (web-frontend-builder produces apps/web/ scaffold)
rows.approach: Three siblings with parallel responsibilities. Stack dispatch logic is uniform; body prompts diverge by scope.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Single builder that dispatches all scopes
rejected_alternatives.reason: Scope-specific tool requirements diverge (mobile needs EAS Build; web needs Playwright); separate agents let each lock down its surface.

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/{tester,reviewer,security}.md — Mode B quality agents. Tester: hybrid TDD edge-cases + integration + Playwright/Maestro, 80% coverage, write-test-only. Reviewer: 8-dimension review (architecture, security, compliance, maintainability, a11y, perf, brief-delivery, design-conformance), stack-aware. Security: OWASP+CWE+ASVS L1, PM-flagged features only, P0/P1/P2 with CWE IDs.
steps: Tester frontmatter: write-test-only enforcement noted (no source edits)
Reviewer stack-aware behavior: loads .claude/skills/agents/<scope>/<slug>/SKILL.md §Review block additively
Security dispatched only on PM-flagged features (row 040 pm-mode-tasks emits security_sensitive: true|false per feature)
rows.approach: Three quality gates with distinct authority. Tester = test code authoring; Reviewer = comprehensive merge gate; Security = specialist on flagged features.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Security review needs OWASP/CWE depth that bloats reviewer prompt; specialist split keeps each prompt focused.

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/{bug-fixer,systemic-fixer}.md — Mode B fix agents. Bug-fixer: smallest-diff narrow patch, bug-class discriminator drives shape. Systemic-fixer: cross-file root-cause for systemic bugs, suspects build pipeline first, multi-file authorized.
steps: Bug-fixer allowed-tools restricted (Read, Edit, Bash for test re-run); no Write/MultiEdit (force surgical edits)
Both reference protected-files-policy.md (row 025) in body prompt at execution time
rows.approach: Tool-surface asymmetry encodes the diff-size invariant: bug-fixer can't physically make wide changes. Systemic-fixer has the surface but is dispatched only on cluster/pattern bugs (per cluster-bugs.ts in Phase 3).

rejected_alternatives.approach: Single fix agent that decides scope dynamically
rejected_alternatives.reason: Tool restriction is a stronger lever than prompt guidance; bug-fixer literally cannot violate the narrow-diff invariant.

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/{perceptual-reviewer,walkthrough-reviewer}.md — Mode B vision-LLM judges (Sonnet). Perceptual: mockup PNG vs live PNG → structured discrepancies, one per screen per fix-loop. Walkthrough: sequenced screenshots + network + console → behavioral findings (dup-request, no-op controls, broken nav, theme inconsistency, silent failures), one per fix-loop.
steps: Author 2 agent files (model: sonnet for vision capability)
Both produce findings only — NOT fixers (handoff to bug-fixer/systemic-fixer)
Both receive upstream tier findings (parity → perceptual; perceptual → walkthrough) to avoid duplication
rows.approach: Vision-LLM judging is per-Tier; cascading findings between tiers stops duplicate work. Sonnet's vision is sufficient and cheaper than Opus for judging.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Cost overkill; Sonnet 4.6 vision rubric calibrates well per Phase 2 feat-068/069.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Let reviewers fix what they find
rejected_alternatives.reason: Separation: judges find, fixers fix. Same agent role-switching erodes review skepticism.

expected_outcomes: Output schemas referenced by row 030 schemas exist

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/git-agent.md — owns worktree lifecycle + branch mgmt + merge-to-main + conflict routing. Operations: bootstrap, checkout-feature, close-feature, resolve-conflict-handoff, emergency-abort. Boundary-only; never runs inline.
steps: Author agent file with allowed-tools (Bash for git ops only; no Write/Edit on source)
Define op contracts in schemas/git-agent.schema.json (row 031 covers this)
Smoke: bootstrap creates .claude/worktrees/feat-001/, checkout-feature switches HEAD
rows.approach: Centralizing git ops in one agent stops accidental git state from leaking into builder/tester contexts. Boundary-only invocation enforces that.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Let each builder do its own git commits
rejected_alternatives.reason: Phase 2 saw builders commit half-built features; centralized commits gate on close-feature verification.

rows.description: Author .claude/agents/{lessons-agent,agent-expert,html-verifier}.md — learning-agent / agent-expert / Haiku html-verifier mechanisms (finishing Phase 2's [UNFINISHED] items per scaffolding 19-032b, 24-037, 26-039).
steps: lessons-agent: captures error-patterns + solutions; updates CLAUDE.md, agent memory across global/project/agent scopes
agent-expert: meta-agent; detects repeating patterns across feature_list.json + LESSONS.md; authors new skills/agents; deposits in library
html-verifier (Haiku model): Layer 6 of output-contracts; validates HTML structure, token usage, primitive adherence post-UI-Designer
Author 3 agent files; smoke each against a synthetic input
rows.approach: These three were [UNFINISHED] in Phase 2's scaffolding. Rebuild includes them as first-class because they are the factory's adaptation loop (lessons → patterns → new skills) and Layer 6 of output-contracts (html-verifier).

rejected_alternatives.approach: Defer agent-expert until factory has months of LESSONS data
rejected_alternatives.reason: Without it, lessons don't compound into new skills; the factory plateaus. Better to ship a low-frequency loop on day one.

rows.description: Author 3 backend stack-skill packs at .claude/skills/agents/back-end/{node-fastify,node-trpc-nest,python-fastapi}/SKILL.md. Each: canonical layout, dep pins (refreshed 2026-04-30+), testing setup, §Review block for reviewer agent.
steps: Refresh dep pins to current versions (Fastify 5+, NestJS 10+, FastAPI 0.115+)
Smoke each against a hello-world scaffold
rows.approach: Stack skills are the polymorphism mechanism for builders. One pack per supported framework; builders dispatch by name.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Per-framework idioms diverge wildly; mega-skill bloats every builder context window.

expected_outcomes: All 3 packs present with valid frontmatter
Each smoke produces a working hello-world

rows.description: Author 2 frontend stack-skill packs (.claude/skills/agents/front-end/{react-next,svelte-kit}/SKILL.md) + 1 mobile (.claude/skills/agents/mobile/expo-rn/SKILL.md). Each: layout, dep pins, testing, §Review.
steps: Refresh pins (Next 14+ App Router, React 19, SvelteKit 2, Svelte 5, Expo 52+, RN 0.77+)
Smoke each: react-next produces apps/web/, svelte-kit produces apps/web/, expo-rn produces apps/mobile/
rows.approach: Mirrors row 043 for frontend + mobile.

rejected_alternatives.reason: PM stack-picking needs at least 2 options to make a non-trivial choice; svelte-kit's small footprint matters for some clients.

rows.description: Author 8 vendor stack-skill stubs (.claude/skills/agents/vendor/{calcom-embed,mux-player-react,next-sanity,plausible-analytics,react-email,resend-transactional,sanity-studio,turnstile-widget}/SKILL.md) + 1 vendor-skill template (_template/SKILL.md) for adding new vendors.
steps: Author 8 vendor SKILL.md stubs with frontmatter + §Layout + §Auth + §Smoke + §Review skeletons
Author _template/SKILL.md as the canonical shape new vendor skills clone from
Each stub clearly marked WIP; passes:true only after a future row promotes them to full
rows.approach: Phase 2 left these as stubs; rebuild matches that scope. Full vendor integrations are out-of-scope for the factory rebuild — each vendor gets a promote-to-full row when a project actually needs it.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Fully implement all 8 vendor integrations now
rejected_alternatives.reason: Vendor APIs change; integrations should be implemented per-need with current API docs. Stubs reserve the namespace + shape.
Builders need to dispatch by slug; missing slug = silent fallback to generic implementation that may violate vendor's TOS or auth model.

expected_outcomes: Template clones to a new vendor in <2 min

rows.description: Author 7 analyzer subscope skills (.claude/skills/analyze/{research,styles,assets,inspirations,flows,screens,integrations}.md). Consumed by analyst's sub-workers.
steps: Each subscope has clear input (brief, prior subscope outputs) + clear output (one or more docs/analysis/ files)
Smoke /analyze 3 against test-app and verify all 7 outputs land
rows.approach: Subscopes are the analyst's modular decomposition. Each is callable independently for /analyze --skip-research mode.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Inline all subscopes into analyst.md body
rejected_alternatives.reason: Bloats agent prompt; subscope files let analyst reference them additively per dispatch.

rows.description: Author scaffolding/ docs: master index (000-scaffolding-index.md) + the 4 Phase 2 [UNFINISHED] active docs finished (19-032b html-verifier wiring, 24-037 lessons-agent wiring, 25-040 app-store-compliance, 26-039 agent-expert wiring) + port the other 4 active docs (10-034 output-contracts, 12-026 turborepo, 13-027 shared-packages, monorepo bootstrap).
steps: Port master index from Phase 2 with Tier 1-4 + Phase A-E structure
Finish 4 [UNFINISHED] active scaffolding docs (link agent files from rows 042, 045)
Verify scaffolding/ index lists all docs with correct tier
rows.approach: Scaffolding docs are factory-internal blueprints — they describe how the factory builds, not what a project ships. Finishing the 4 [UNFINISHED] docs eliminates Phase 2's biggest debt.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip scaffolding docs; rely on feature_list.json alone
rejected_alternatives.reason: Scaffolding docs encode design rationale (why 7-layer output contract, why turborepo); feature_list.json captures tasks but not the architectural why.

expected_outcomes: All active scaffolding docs present (no [UNFINISHED] flags)
Master index lists each at correct tier

rows.description: HUMAN: walk the full Phase 0 factory tree; spot-check 10 random feature_list.json rows for plan-parity vs shipped code; confirm 5-agent /analyze smoke fanout. Sign off Phase 0 closure.
steps: tree -L 3 .claude/ schemas/ packages/ scaffolding/ — confirm expected shape
Run /phase-gate (invokes retro subagent on 5 random rows)
Spot-check 5 additional rows manually (read row + evidence + diff)
Smoke /analyze 3 on test-app; observe 5 parallel workers
Approve in evidence/phase0-step-048-result.txt; git tag phase-0-done
rows.approach: Phase 0 closure gate. The retro agent samples; human spot-checks reinforce. Tag enables future Phase 1 row reference.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip retro; trust per-row evidence files
rejected_alternatives.reason: Per-row evidence is local; retro catches inter-row drift (e.g. row 022 invoke-agent shape doesn't match row 031 contract package types).

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/stages-array.ts — canonical Mode A stage list with real Zod schemas (resolve Phase 2's [UNFINISHED] z.unknown placeholders per task-034b). 13 stages: analyze, skills-audit-design, mockups, stylesheet, stylesheet-primitives, screens, visual-review, user-flows, architect, pm, skills-audit-build, register-mcp-build, git-agent-bootstrap.
steps: Port Phase 2 stages-array.ts; replace each z.unknown with the concrete output schema from packages/orchestrator-contracts (row phase0-step-031)
Each stage entry: { slug, agentSlug, outputSchema, gateBefore?, gateAfter?, retryTier }
Unit test: each stage's outputSchema validates a known-good fixture from Phase 2's archived runs
rows.approach: stages-array is the spine of Mode A. Schema-level wiring (not z.unknown) is what lets the pipeline detect bad agent output before downstream consumers crash.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Phase 2 carried z.unknown for 6 months; in that time 12 bugs traced to bad agent outputs passing the placeholder gate. Real schemas catch it at the dispatch boundary.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/stage-runner.ts — runs one slash-command stage through SDK, validates output schema, retries on Layer-5 failure (max 3). Enforces budget pre-query, records spend post-return.
steps: Wire budget-tracker (phase0-step-019) pre-query check + post-return record
Unit test: schema-fail triggers retry; 3rd fail propagates
rows.approach: Single-stage executor. All Mode A stages flow through it. Budget + retry threading is the same for every stage.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Boilerplate proliferation; single runner with stage-config keeps retry/budget logic in one place.

expected_outcomes: Budget pre-query throws when over cap

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/pipeline.ts — Mode A orchestrator. Walks STAGES array, fires each via runStage(), waits on gates (file-drop watcher), persists state. Integrates brief-coverage gate post-/pm.
steps: SaveContextFn: log-only MVP (operator runs /save-context manually for now)
Unit test: pipeline run with all gates auto-approved completes; with one gate blocked, pauses correctly
rows.approach: Sequential walker over STAGES. File-drop gates keep the model out of the gate-decision loop (operator drops file when ready).

rejected_alternatives.approach: Use an HTTP gate server (no files)
rejected_alternatives.reason: Phase 2 MVP'd HTTP and reverted to file-drop — files survive crashes and don't need a long-running server process.

expected_outcomes: Pipeline runs 13 stages end-to-end on test-app

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/project-state.ts — detects which Mode A stages completed by primary output-file presence. Used by --resume-from-stage to validate the resume point.
steps: Per-stage detector: detectOne(stageSlug) checks the canonical output file (e.g. analyze → docs/analysis/shared/research_brief.yaml)
Unit test: synthetic project with 5 stages done → bitmap matches
rows.approach: Conservative detection: output file exists OR doesn't. No content validation (schemas already do that). Keeps resume logic simple.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Duplicates schema validation; if output file is malformed, stage-runner will re-validate when resume re-runs the consumer stage.

expected_outcomes: Detector handles missing files (returns false, no crash)

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/kit-change-request-detour.ts — design-phase detour when agent requests new kit component. Invokes PM (--mode=kit-change-request), reruns /stylesheet, optionally reruns /architect. Mutation of retry counter; escalates on exhaust (2-per-pipeline cap).
steps: Wire retry-counters: cap=2 kit-change-requests per pipeline run
Unit test: 3rd detour throws + suggests human escalation
rows.approach: Mid-design corrections happen — UI Designer requests a Tabs primitive that wasn't in initial kit. Detour lets the pipeline self-heal without restart, capped to prevent runaway iteration.

rejected_alternatives.approach: No detour; force operator restart on kit gaps
rejected_alternatives.reason: Operator restart loses Mode A state; detour preserves it and is reversibly capped.

expected_outcomes: Detour completes with updated kit + screens

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/visual-review-retry.ts — retry loop for /visual-review error-severity violations. Regenerates screen per error, re-runs visual-review. Capped 3 per screen (independent of Layer 5). Feeds needsHumanReview[] to Gate 4.
steps: Wire retry-counters: per-screen cap=3, independent counter
Unit test: 3 fails → screen lands in needsHumanReview[]
rows.approach: Visual review is rubric-fuzzy; 3 retries usually resolve typographic and layout misses. Past 3, human eyes are cheaper than more model dispatches.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Layer-5 is dispatch-failure retry; visual-review retry is content-failure retry. Distinct semantics → distinct caps.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/gate-server-lifecycle.ts — file-drop gate machinery. waitForGateDecision polls FS for gate-{n}-approved.txt / gate-{n}-approved-{featureId}.txt. Parses directives (proceed | revise | reject | abort | defer). MVP no-op HTTP server.
steps: Implement directive parser: file contents are 'proceed' OR 'revise: <reason>' OR 'reject: <reason>' OR 'abort' OR 'defer'
Unit test: each directive parses; missing file = wait
rows.approach: File-drop is the simplest possible gate UX: operator writes a one-line file, orchestrator detects it. Survives operator restarts, network blips, anything.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Adds infra dependency; file-drop works on a single dev box without network.

expected_outcomes: Wait loop terminates within 1s of file appearance

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/brief-coverage-gate.ts — post-/pm gate. Spawns scripts/audit-brief-coverage.mjs; parses exit code + BriefCoverageOutput JSON. No-op + warn when brief.capabilities catalog DNE (legacy projects).
steps: Unit test: catalog with all features covered → pass; missing coverage → fail with structured report
rows.approach: Catches PM-generated tasks.yaml that drops brief features. The audit script is small + standalone; the gate just wraps its exit code.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Manual brief-coverage check at human gate
rejected_alternatives.reason: Easy to miss in a 100-feature tasks.yaml; automated audit is exhaustive.

expected_outcomes: Coverage gap detected on synthetic incomplete tasks.yaml
Legacy project (no capabilities) warns but doesn't fail

rows.description: Wire /analyze stage — first Mode A stage. Dispatches analyst agent (phase0-step-035) with 5-worker parallel fan-out. Outputs: docs/analysis/shared/{competitors,voice,inspirations,formats}.md + per-direction folders + research_brief.yaml. Flags: --style-count N, --platforms, --skip-research.
steps: Author .claude/skills/analyze/SKILL.md as the operator entry point
Skill body dispatches analyst agent with operator flags
Verify output schema matches stages-array's analyze entry (row 001)
Smoke /analyze 3 on test-app — verify all expected files land
rows.approach: Skill is a thin wrapper around the agent dispatch. All analyst logic lives in the agent body (authored at execution time).

rejected_alternatives.reason: Skill provides flag parsing + dispatch context; calling agent raw bypasses operator UX.

rows.description: HUMAN: inspect /analyze outputs (research, style options, mood, requirements, per-direction folders, research_brief.yaml). Confirm direction-K folders are visually + tonally distinct. Sign off Gate 1 to proceed to /mockups.
steps: Confirm N directions are genuinely distinct (not 5 reskins of one direction)
rows.approach: First content-quality gate. Bad analyze output cascades into bad mockups, bad ui-kit, bad screens — catch it here.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Aesthetic distinctness is human judgment; current vision-LLM rubrics conflate distinctness with quality.

expected_outcomes: Operator signs off OR rejects with structured reason

rows.description: Wire /skills-audit --scope=design stage. Dispatches skills-agent (phase0-step-036); confirms design-stage MCPs (playwright, icons8, unsplash, chrome-devtools, image-generator) registered; flags missing.
steps: --scope=design checks for playwright, icons8, unsplash, chrome-devtools, image-generator
Idempotent: re-run with same state produces same output
rows.approach: Audit is read-only by default. Operator decides whether to register missing MCPs (rows 020 register-mcp-build covers the build-scope).

rejected_alternatives.reason: MCP installs may require auth tokens or user-facing OAuth; surface the gap, let operator decide.

expected_outcomes: Missing MCPs flagged but don't fail the stage

rows.description: Wire /mockups stage. Dispatches ui-designer (phase0-step-036) to generate M (app × style) mockup variants from N analyzer style options. Operator picks one style at Gate 2 via /pick-style → populates docs/selected-style.json.
steps: /mockups N --direction K (optional) re-renders cheap-image variations of a single direction without re-running /analyze
Emit mockup HTML grid using templates/mockups-index-template.html
/pick-style writes docs/selected-style.json (schema lean-marketing/picked-direction v1.0 from row phase0-step-030)
rows.approach: Mockups stage is content-generative + cheap (image-only models). Pick-style is the operator's commitment moment — locks the visual binding.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Brand fit is a judgment call; auto-pick produces designs nobody owns.

rows.description: HUMAN: review mockup grid; run /pick-style K to commit Gate 2. Confirm selected-style.json captures the operator's choice.
rows.approach: The one hard HITL gate in Mode A. Commits the visual identity; downstream stylesheet/screens depend on this commit being final.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Defer style commit until after stylesheet
rejected_alternatives.reason: Stylesheet IS the commitment artifact; un-committing requires rebuilding ui-kit which is 10× more expensive than re-running /mockups.

rows.description: Wire /stylesheet stage + stylesheet-primitives substage. UI Designer scaffolds packages/ui-kit/ with tokens (color, spacing, typography), primitive components (Button, Card, Input, …), patterns, layouts, accessibility baseline. Versioned ui-kit@1.0.0 + CHANGELOG. Stylesheet-primitives substage authors the primitive component code.
steps: /stylesheet dispatches ui-designer with selected-style + design dials
rows.approach: Two-stage: stylesheet creates structure (tokens, layouts, contract); primitives populates the components. Versioning at v1.0.0 lets gate-3 signoff lock the binding.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Tokens + primitives have different dispatch shapes (tokens = small JSON; primitives = lots of TSX). Splitting keeps each within agent context window.

expected_outcomes: All ESLint plugin rules pass on generated primitives

rows.description: Wire /screens stage. UI Designer composes screens from ui-kit primitives. Reads docs/analysis/{platform}/screens.json + ui-kit. For each screen, emits HTML with data-kit-* attrs. Emits docs/screens/{platform}/*.html + docs/screens-manifest.json.
steps: Dispatches ui-designer in screen-composition mode (reads screen state metadata)
Each screen file uses data-kit-* attrs (consumed by builder agents in Phase 2)
Manifest validates against schemas/screens.schema.json (discriminated-union batch vs single-screen retry)
rows.approach: Screens HTML is the design→build handoff artifact. data-kit-* attrs are the soft binding builders consume to compose runtime components from same ui-kit.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip data-kit-* attrs; let builders re-derive
rejected_alternatives.reason: Re-derivation gap is where Phase 2 saw 30+ parity bugs; explicit attrs make the contract machine-checkable.

rows.description: Wire /visual-review stage. UI Designer's self-review layer. Renders composed screens via Playwright; screenshots; applies rubric (typography scale, color contrast, alignment, hierarchy, consistency with mockups). Flags failures → designer iterates via visual-review-retry (row 006).
steps: Dispatches ui-designer in self-review mode; uses Playwright via MCP
Wire visual-review-retry (row 006) per-screen cap=3; failed screens → needsHumanReview[]
rows.approach: Self-critique catches obvious slop before human review burns operator attention. Per-screen retry quarantine keeps unfixable screens from blocking the whole pipeline.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip self-review; rely on perceptual-reviewer in Phase 3
rejected_alternatives.reason: Perceptual-reviewer is downstream of build; visual-review catches design-time issues that would otherwise force a re-stylesheet detour after build.

rows.description: Wire /user-flows stage. Reads docs/analysis/{platform}/flows.md + screens-manifest. Generates visual flow diagram (Mermaid or SVG) showing screen transitions + decision points. Binds to Gate 4 signoff.
steps: Author .claude/skills/user-flows/SKILL.md (Phase 2 called it user-flows-generator; align name with stages-array)
Dispatches ui-designer or a dedicated flows generator with screens-manifest
rows.approach: Flow diagrams give human reviewers a navigable picture of the product before builders start. Mermaid for text-editable + SVG for richer visuals.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip flow generation; rely on screen list
rejected_alternatives.reason: Screen list doesn't show transitions; flows catch missing nav (e.g. no path back to home) that screen list hides.

rows.description: HUMAN: review stylesheet + screens + visual-review reports + user-flows. Sign off Gate 3 (design-system commit) and Gate 4 (flow-completeness commit). Write signoff.json (schemas/signoff.schema.json).
steps: Open packages/ui-kit/CHANGELOG.md + ui-kit primitives gallery (if generated)
Read docs/visual-review/*.json — confirm all errors resolved OR explicitly accepted
Walk user-flows SVG — confirm no missing transitions
Author docs/signoff.json with reviews[], visualReviewReportHash, uiKitVersion
rows.approach: Combined Gate 3+4 inspection. Both gate the same downstream consumer (architect); reviewing them together saves operator context-switching.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Operator is already in design-review mode; combining is workflow-aligned.

rows.description: Wire /architect stage. Reads brief.md + signoff.json + ui-kit version + analysis. Produces architecture.yaml + .env.example + credentials-checklist.md + deployment-checklist.md. Hash-detect: re-run emits credentials-diff vs prior.
steps: Hash-detect: if prior architecture.yaml exists, emit credentials-diff section listing new/changed env vars
rows.approach: Architect is single-shot per pipeline run unless hash changes. Credentials diff is the operator's checklist for what new secrets need to be set up before Mode B.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Forces re-validating env vars even on no-op re-runs; hash-detect avoids the busywork.

expected_outcomes: Credentials diff appears on re-run with changed inputs

rows.description: HUMAN: review architect output (architecture.yaml + credentials-checklist + deployment-checklist). Configure credentials (.env entries, vendor accounts, deploy targets). Sign off Gate 5.
steps: Walk credentials checklist; create vendor accounts; populate .env
Confirm deployment-checklist preconditions met (DNS, hosting, CDN, etc.)
rows.approach: Credentials gate. PM dispatch needs env vars resolved (e.g. database URL) before tasks.yaml can describe migrations correctly. Operator must do real-world setup here.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Builders need env at install/test time; failing late wastes a Mode B run.

rows.description: Wire /pm --mode=tasks stage. Dispatches project-manager (phase0-step-036) to decompose requirements + architecture into tasks.yaml v2 (features[], agent_sequence[], task graph). Enforces feature-grouping heuristics (shared flow, catalogue entry, integration).
steps: Output: docs/tasks.yaml v2 validates against schemas/tasks.schema.json
Each feature has agent_sequence[] (builder, security?, tester, reviewer)
Smoke against test-app; verify cycle-free DAG
rows.approach: PM produces the executable feature graph. Mode B reads this verbatim; correctness here is critical.

rejected_alternatives.reason: PM applies grouping heuristics that catch shared-state features (e.g. login + signup share auth flow); manual decomposition misses these reliably.

rows.description: Wire /skills-audit --scope=build + /register-mcp-build stages. skills-agent confirms build-stage skills + MCPs (per architecture.yaml.tooling.mcp_servers[]). register-mcp-servers installs missing MCPs into .mcp.json.
steps: Idempotent: re-run with all MCPs present is a no-op
rows.approach: Pair: audit reads-only; register writes. Operator confirms register intent (some MCPs need OAuth).

rejected_alternatives.reason: MCP installs may launch OAuth flows; surface intent to operator first.

rows.description: Wire /git-agent --op=bootstrap stage. git-agent (phase0-step-041) creates monorepo skeleton (turborepo + pnpm-workspace + .claude/worktrees/ dir + initial commit). Final Mode A stage.
steps: Wire git-agent bootstrap op into stages-array as final Mode A stage
Bootstrap creates: apps/{web,mobile,api,admin}/ stubs, packages/{types,ui-kit,api-client,utils}/, turbo.json, pnpm-workspace.yaml
rows.approach: Bootstrap is the design→build handoff; runs once. After this, every feature gets its own worktree via /git-agent checkout-feature in Mode B.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Manual step is forgotten; auto-bootstrap eliminates the gap.

expected_outcomes: pnpm install succeeds with native bindings

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/{idea,idea-list,idea-promote}/SKILL.md — brainstorm/idea workflow detours. /idea expands a one-liner into brief §11 catalogue + feature sketches + integration assumptions. /idea-list enumerates open ideas. /idea-promote graduates an idea into a brief §11 entry + tasks.yaml row.
steps: Port Phase 2 idea + idea-promote SKILL.md; resolve [UNFINISHED] idea-list (canonical scope: enumerates docs/ideas/*.md with status)
/idea-promote updates brief §11 + appends tasks.yaml feature; mid-Mode-B safe (won't disrupt running graph)
rows.approach: Mid-design + mid-build expansion mechanism. Without this, operators have to restart pipelines to add features they discover mid-flight.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Defer until first operator hits the need
rejected_alternatives.reason: Phase 2 had this; rebuild parity matters. Ideas surface routinely during /analyze review.

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/{plan-refactor,plan-archive,plan-search,plan-status}/SKILL.md — supplementary planning skills (refactor mini-plans, archive/search active plans, status dashboard).
steps: Port Phase 2 plan-refactor + plan-archive + plan-search + plan-status SKILL.md
plan-refactor scopes the refactor (files, libs), predicts impact (features to re-review), can block downstream via DAG
plan-status reports in-progress / blocked / review-pending counts
rows.approach: Operator power tools. Lower priority than core stage skills but used routinely once factory is in production.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip; plans/ can be managed manually
rejected_alternatives.reason: Skills enforce frontmatter shape and dependency awareness; manual mgmt loses both.

expected_outcomes: /plan-status reports correctly on a synthetic plans/ dir

rows.description: HUMAN: end-to-end smoke Mode A on test-app. Walk all 13 stages, 5 HITL gates, produced artifacts. Sign off Phase 1 closure; git tag phase-1-done.
steps: At each gate (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), inspect outputs and approve via file drop
Confirm final state: brief.md, analysis/, mockups/, ui-kit/, screens/, user-flows/, signoff.json, architecture.yaml, tasks.yaml v2, .env populated, monorepo bootstrapped
Run /phase-gate (invokes retro on 5 random Phase 1 rows)
rows.approach: Phase 1 closure gate. The smoke run validates inter-stage handoffs that per-row tests miss.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip e2e smoke; rely on row-level tests
rejected_alternatives.reason: Stage handoffs (e.g. selected-style.json → ui-kit tokens) are exactly where inter-row drift hides.

expected_outcomes: Smoke run completes without unplanned operator intervention

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/tasks-loader.ts — load + schema-validate docs/tasks.yaml v2 (PM output from phase1-step-021). Throws on missing / malformed / version-mismatch.
steps: Unit test: malformed yaml → structured error; valid yaml → parsed object
rows.approach: First call in Mode B; fail fast on bad input so the operator sees the error before any agent dispatch.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Lazy validation can surface a malformed feature 90% into the graph; eager validation surfaces it at start.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/feature-graph.ts — Mode B feature executor. Walks TasksV2 array, dispatches agents per task via invokeAgent, commits worktree, installs deps per package.json change, gates on pr-review if enabled (Gate 6). Resolves Phase 2's [UNFINISHED] perceptual integration + fine-grained pausing.
steps: Finish perceptual-review integration: hand verifier results to bug-fixer dispatches
Implement fine-grained pause: pause-on-feature-boundary AND pause-mid-feature-after-current-agent
Unit test: 3-feature DAG executes; pause at feature 2 resumes correctly
rows.approach: The single Mode B driver. Per-feature loop: checkout worktree → dispatch agent_sequence → close-feature merge → next feature. Fine-grained pause is the key UX win over Phase 2's coarse mid-graph pause.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Operators sometimes need to inspect mid-feature state (e.g. after builder, before tester); fine-grained pause supports that.

rows.description: Extend orchestrator/src/invoke-agent.ts for Mode B per-feature dispatch. Add: worktree-aware spawn (cwd = .claude/worktrees/<featureId>/), bug-fix context injection from bug-fix-context.ts (Phase 3 dep, stub now), tester-diff-audit gate post-tester.
steps: Extend invoke-agent.ts (built in phase0-step-022) with Mode-B dispatch path
Stub bug-fix-context.ts integration (real impl in phase3-step-008)
Wire tester-diff-audit guard post-tester dispatch (real impl in phase3-step-009)
Unit test: dispatch with cwd override; tester output flagged when anti-pattern present
rows.approach: Mode-B extends the dispatch core authored in Phase 0 — keeps the dispatch chokepoint single. Phase 3 modules stubbed so Phase 2 can land independently.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Two dispatch paths = two bug surfaces; one extension keeps invariants centralized.

rows.description: Wire git-agent operations for Mode B: bootstrap (Mode A end), checkout-feature (start of per-feature loop), close-feature (post-reviewer merge), resolve-conflict-handoff (on merge conflict), emergency-abort (rollback worktree on failure).
steps: Extend feature-graph to call git-agent at op boundaries
checkout-feature: creates .claude/worktrees/<featureId>/ on feat/<id> branch off main
close-feature: rebase + merge to main + delete worktree (unless --keep-worktrees flag)
resolve-conflict-handoff: emits docs/conflicts/<featureId>.md + pauses; operator resolves; gate file unblocks
emergency-abort: rolls back worktree changes, marks feature failed in tasks.yaml
rows.approach: Git-agent is the single git authority. Each op is invoked at a precise boundary in feature-graph; orchestrator never runs git directly.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Inline git scatters destructive ops (rollback, merge) across the loop; centralized agent keeps the safety boundary.

expected_outcomes: All 5 ops invoke from feature-graph at correct boundaries

rows.description: Wire protected-files guard (phase0-step-024) post-each-dispatch in feature-graph. On violation: rollback worktree to pre-dispatch state, mark dispatch failed, increment retry counter, log incident.
steps: Call verifyProtectedFiles() after every agent dispatch returns in feature-graph
On violation: git reset --hard pre-dispatch-sha; mark task status=protected-files-violation
Increment retry counter (cap=2 per task; 3rd violation = task hard-fail)
Unit test: synthetic agent deletes postcss.config.mjs → rollback restores
rows.approach: Hard rollback enforced at the dispatch boundary, not on commit. Catches violations before they enter feature branch history.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Too late — feature branch history is dirty; rollback loses unrelated good work.

rows.description: Wire builder dispatch per agent_sequence. Per task: invoke web-frontend-builder / mobile-frontend-builder / backend-builder based on task.scope. Builder reads architecture.yaml + screens-manifest + ui-kit; generates code + happy-path tests; commits worktree.
steps: feature-graph.dispatchBuilder(task): picks builder agent from task.scope
Builder dispatched via invoke-agent (phase2-step-003) with cwd = worktree
Verify post-dispatch: pnpm install ran if package.json changed; tests file present
Smoke 1-feature graph with each of 3 builder scopes
rows.approach: Builders are scope-polymorphic; dispatch logic picks by task.scope. Hybrid TDD 60% means builder ships happy-path; tester fills edge-cases.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Per-scope tool surface diverges (mobile needs EAS; backend needs DB tools); separate dispatch keeps each focused.

expected_outcomes: All 3 scopes dispatch + produce expected output
pnpm install runs on package.json change

rows.description: Wire security dispatch — only when PM flags feature as security_sensitive: true. Security agent runs OWASP Top 10 + CWE Top 25 + ASVS L1 against builder diff. Emits findings with severity (P0/P1/P2) + CWE IDs + retryTargets[]. Runs post-builders, pre-reviewer.
steps: On P0 finding: route to bug-fixer in same iteration (retry cap=2)
On P1/P2: file as docs/bugs.yaml entry (Phase 3 consumes)
rows.approach: Security specializes where reviewer is generalist. Conditional dispatch saves cost on non-sensitive features (typical ratio: 30% of features flagged).

rejected_alternatives.reason: Cost overhead on UI-only features that have no auth/data surface; PM-flagging is the right filter.

expected_outcomes: P0 routes to bug-fixer immediately; P1/P2 to bugs.yaml

rows.description: Wire tester dispatch per agent_sequence. Tester runs hybrid TDD: trusts builder happy-path tests; adds edge-case unit + integration + E2E (Playwright web / Maestro mobile). Reports coverage ≥80%. Flags genuine product bugs back to builder (max 3 retries per task).
steps: Tester (phase0-step-038) reads builder diff + reads existing tests; adds edges
If flaggedBugs[].kind === 'product-bug': route to builder retry (cap=3)
tester-diff-audit (phase3-step-009 stub now) flags 6 anti-patterns; blocks dispatch if hits + no genuineProductBugs[] override
rows.approach: Hybrid TDD splits authoring authority by test type. Coverage threshold + anti-pattern audit catch the most common tester failures (cheating via assertion loosening).

rejected_alternatives.approach: Tester writes all tests (no builder happy-path)
rejected_alternatives.reason: Builder's happy-path tests serve as in-context spec; removing them increases builder hallucination.

expected_outcomes: Genuine product bug routes to builder retry

rows.description: Wire reviewer dispatch — last agent before close-feature merge. Reviewer walks 8 dimensions (architecture, security, compliance, maintainability, a11y, perf, brief-delivery, design-conformance) against branch diff. Stack-aware (loads stack-skill §Review additively). Emits ReviewerOutput with overallVerdict + retryTargets[].
steps: feature-graph.dispatchReviewer(task) — after builders + optional security + tester
Reviewer (phase0-step-038) reads branch diff + stack-skill §Review block
Output: docs/reviews/<feature>-<task>.json with verdict (approved | needs-revision | blocked) + retryTargets
approved → proceed to close-feature; needs-revision → route to retryTargets (cap=2); blocked → escalate to human
rows.approach: Reviewer is the comprehensive merge gate. Stack-aware loading lets per-framework conventions stack with universal rubric.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Reviewer + fixer in one agent loses skepticism (same context wrote and reviewed); separate dispatch enforces fresh-eyes.

rows.description: Wire close-feature merge. On reviewer approval: git-agent close-feature op rebases feat/<id> onto main, runs pre-merge checks (pnpm typecheck + lint + test on rebased branch), merges with --no-ff (preserves feature history), deletes worktree (unless --keep-worktrees).
steps: Rebase + pre-merge checks; on fail → resolve-conflict-handoff or emergency-abort
Merge with --no-ff message: 'feat(<feature-slug>): <one-liner from task description>'
Optional pr-review gate (Gate 6) blocks merge until gate-6-approved-{featureId}.txt drops
rows.approach: Close-feature is the single merge boundary; PR-review gate optional for high-stakes features. Worktree deletion frees disk after merge.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Adds operator latency on every feature; --require-pr-review flag is opt-in.

expected_outcomes: Pre-merge check fail routes to conflict handoff
PR-review gate honored when flag set

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/start-build/SKILL.md — Mode B entry. Reads docs/tasks.yaml v2. Invokes feature-graph driver. Flags: --dry-run, --max-concurrent N, --require-pr-review, --resume-feature-graph, --keep-worktrees.
steps: Skill body: validates tasks.yaml exists, parses flags, invokes runFeatureGraph from cli-runner
--dry-run: walk tasks.yaml without dispatching agents (verify DAG)
rows.approach: Operator's Mode B entry. Flag set covers the common UX needs (resume, dry-run, concurrency tuning, pr-review).

rejected_alternatives.reason: Different runs need different flag combos; aggregate flags reduce composability.

rows.description: HUMAN: review start-build dispatch plan on test-app (--dry-run output). Confirm feature ordering, agent_sequence per feature, security_sensitive flags, parallel-worker plan. Approve actual run.
steps: Read produced docs/dispatch-plan.md: features in topological order, agent_sequence[] per feature, parallel-worker bucketing
Spot-check: at least one feature should have security in sequence (test PM's flagging heuristic)
Run /start-build (no dry-run); record observations in evidence/phase2-step-012-result.txt
rows.approach: Dispatch-plan inspection catches PM bugs that only surface as cascading failures during real run. Cheap to inspect; expensive to debug post-hoc.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Per-feature dispatch costs add up; catching a PM bug before $50 of agent dispatch is high-leverage.

expected_outcomes: Operator approves plan OR catches a bug + files plan-bug

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/{pause-build,dag-status,quota-status}/SKILL.md — Mode B operator skills. pause-build halts mid-graph; dag-status reports live DAG state; quota-status reports cost/token spend with cap thresholds.
steps: Port Phase 2 pause-build, dag-status, quota-status SKILL.md
pause-build: drops .claude/state/{runId}/pause-requested.txt; orchestrator detects + flushes state
dag-status: reads tasks.yaml + feature-contexts; reports running/queued/failed/retries/ETA
quota-status: reads budget-tracker snapshot + ~/.claude/models.yaml caps; warns at 80%, alerts at 95%
rows.approach: Three operator power tools. Each is read-only or trivially writes a flag file; safe to invoke any time.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Status query during active run shouldn't share the start-build CLI subprocess; separate skill = independent invocation.

rows.description: Wire .claude/worktrees/ machinery. Per-feature worktree at .claude/worktrees/<featureId>/ on feat/<featureId> branch. Lazy create + reset --hard per loop iteration. Cleanup on close-feature unless --keep-worktrees.
steps: Implement ensureWorktree(featureId) helper in git-agent op-set
Verify disk reclaim after merge sequence on 3-feature smoke
rows.approach: Per-feature worktrees give each builder/tester/reviewer/security a clean cwd that won't conflict with parallel features.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Parallel feature workers would stomp on each other's files; per-feature isolation is mandatory for --max-concurrent > 1.

rows.description: Wire pause/resume integration in feature-graph. pause checkpoints feature-graph progress (per-feature state, per-task state, retry counters, budget) to .claude/state/{runId}/feature-graph.json. resume loads + reattaches to in-flight workers.
steps: Implement saveFeatureGraphState() called periodically + on pause signal
Implement loadFeatureGraphState() called when --resume-feature-graph flag set
Smoke: pause mid-feature-2 of 3; resume completes feature 2 + 3
rows.approach: Resume preserves in-flight invariants. Without it operators have to restart the whole graph, losing 2 features of agent dispatch budget.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Coarse resume wastes mid-feature work; per-task state lets resume pick up at exact agent boundary.

rows.description: Wire conflict-resolution handoff. On rebase conflict in close-feature: git-agent emits docs/conflicts/<featureId>.md describing conflict + files, pauses pipeline, operator resolves + drops gate-conflict-{featureId}-resolved.txt to continue.
steps: Conflict markdown: branch sha, files, suggested resolution hints, instructions for operator
Operator: resolves locally in worktree, drops gate file, resume continues
Smoke: synthetic conflict on shared file; verify handoff + resume
rows.approach: Conflicts are inherently human judgment. Pipeline doesn't try to auto-resolve; clean handoff to operator with full context.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Agent-resolved conflicts hide intent loss; human resolution preserves authorial decision.

expected_outcomes: Conflict markdown contains all needed context

rows.description: Wire optional PR-review gate (Gate 6). When --require-pr-review is set, close-feature pauses before merge; operator reviews diff in any tool; drops gate-6-approved-{featureId}.txt to allow merge.
steps: Wire gate-server-lifecycle (phase1-step-007) gate 6 in close-feature path
Gate file directive: 'proceed' (merge) | 'revise: <reason>' (back to reviewer with reason) | 'abort' (mark feature failed)
Smoke 1-feature with --require-pr-review: verify pause + each directive
rows.approach: Opt-in human merge gate for high-stakes features (auth, billing, compliance). Default off because most features don't warrant per-merge review.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Latency overhead on every feature; opt-in flag respects operator's risk model per project.

rows.description: HUMAN: after first close-feature on test-app, inspect merged main branch — app builds, tests pass, ui-kit primitives match design, feature works end-to-end. Approve continuation OR file plan-bug.
steps: Switch to main; pnpm install + pnpm build + pnpm test
Start dev server; manually exercise the feature in browser/simulator
If matches expectation: drop gate file 'proceed' for next feature
If diverges: file plan-bug describing divergence; pause-build; investigate
rows.approach: First-feature inspection catches systemic divergences before they cascade across 10+ features. The cost (~5 min operator time) is tiny vs. discovering a builder/PM systemic bug after 10 features built.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Inspect only after all features complete
rejected_alternatives.reason: Systemic bugs discovered late require re-running every feature; first-feature inspection catches them early.

expected_outcomes: First feature works end-to-end OR systemic bug filed

rows.description: Wire dev-server boot from feature-graph (post-close-feature) for in-loop testing. Calls dev-server.bootDevServer() (Phase 3 dep, stubbed now). Coordinates frontend + backend port allocation (NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE).
steps: Stub dev-server.bootDevServer() / teardownDevServer() interfaces in Phase 2 (real impl in phase3-step-002)
feature-graph optional --boot-on-close flag: post-close, boots dev-server, runs reachability ping, teardown
Smoke against test-app: verify ping reaches frontend + backend
rows.approach: Decouple dev-server interface from full Phase 3 implementation. Stub in Phase 2 lets feature-graph compile + smoke without blocking on Phase 3 verifier rows.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Mode B optional boot-on-close is a useful operator UX even without full verifier suite.

rows.description: HUMAN: end-to-end Mode B smoke on test-app's 3-feature tasks.yaml. Walk all close-feature merges + final app state. Sign off Phase 2 closure; git tag phase-2-done.
steps: At each close-feature: inspect main; approve continuation
After final feature: pnpm build + pnpm test + dev-server smoke
rows.approach: Phase 2 closure gate. End-to-end smoke catches integration bugs that per-feature inspection misses (e.g. ui-kit drift across 3 sequential features).

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip end-to-end; per-feature inspection is enough
rejected_alternatives.reason: Inter-feature drift (e.g. one feature ships v1.0 primitive; second uses v1.1 by mistake) only shows end-to-end.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/build-to-spec-verify.ts — orchestrator wrapper for the 5-tier verifier. Shells reachability + synth-flows scripts; runs pre-verify-discriminators, parity, perceptual, walkthrough; aggregates divergences + flow failures; auto-files bug plans.
steps: Port Phase 2 build-to-spec-verify.ts; finish [UNFINISHED] perceptual + walkthrough integration
Aggregate output: docs/build-to-spec/summary.json with per-tier pass/fail + divergences[]
