Auto-file bugs to docs/bugs.yaml (schema phase0-step-030 bugs-yaml.schema.json)
rows.approach: Single orchestrator wraps the 5 tiers; per-tier modules are independently testable. Cascade-skip logic (Tier 4 skipped if Tier 3 found systemic) lives here.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Tier 4-5 are vision-LLM expensive ($0.029/call); skipping on Tier 3 systemic saves ~$5 per failed verification run.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/dev-server.ts — boot/teardown for verifier-needed dev server. Stack-aware backend port (bug-038 lesson), spawn-command resolution (bug-043). Coordinates frontend + backend ports (NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE).
steps: bootDevServer(): detects apps/api/ for co-boot; sets NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE on frontend env
teardownDevServer(): platform-specific (taskkill on Windows, process.kill on POSIX)
Unit test: boot + ping + teardown round-trip on test-app stack
rows.approach: Dev-server is needed by Tiers 2-5. Stack-aware boot keeps the verifier framework-agnostic.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Real Playwright walks need a real server; Tier 2 reachability would be vacuous on a stub.

expected_outcomes: Platform-specific teardown works on Windows + POSIX

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/pre-verify-discriminators.ts — cheap (~10ms) FS checks for known systemic misconfigs (CSS pipeline, Tailwind config, test-seed contract). Each returns DiscriminatorResult or null. Pure functions, no Playwright.
steps: Unit test: each fires on synthetic broken config; returns null on healthy config
rows.approach: Fast pre-flight checks catch systemic bugs cheaper than full Tier 3-5. If pre-verify finds one, skip downstream tiers and route directly to systemic-fixer.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Tier 3 synth-flows would burn $0.50 per case to discover a 10ms-checkable Tailwind config bug.

expected_outcomes: All 3 discriminators fire on broken configs

rows.description: Author Tier 1-3 verifier scripts: scripts/audit-app-reachability.mjs (Tier 2 — boot dev-server, ping each route from screens-manifest) + scripts/synthesize-flow-e2e.mjs (Tier 3 — Playwright per user-flow). Tier 1 (build-sanity) is pnpm build + lint + typecheck shelled.
steps: Author audit-app-reachability.mjs: walk screens-manifest routes, fetch each, record 200/4xx/5xx + load time
Author synthesize-flow-e2e.mjs: walk user-flows-manifest flows, generate Playwright spec per flow, run, record screenshots + console + network
Tier 1 shelled by build-to-spec-verify (`pnpm -r build && pnpm -r lint && pnpm -r typecheck`)
rows.approach: Tier 1-3 cover deterministic verifications (build/reach/flow). Tier 4-5 cover perceptual judgment. Splitting authority keeps each tier's verifier scope-bounded.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Different verifiers have different cost profiles ($0 for Tier 1, $30+ for Tier 5); cascade-skip needs tier separation.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/parity-verify.ts (Tier 4 structural) + audit-pixel-diff.ts (Tier 4 pixel-level). Parity-verify: enumerate mockup HTML, Playwright per-screen, diff kit-skeleton + computed-styles. Audit-pixel-diff: pure-function PNG diff via pixelmatch.
steps: Port both modules verbatim from Phase 2 (resolve [UNFINISHED] parity-verify Phase 2 feat-067)
parity-verify divergences: layout-regrouping, shell-stripping, variant-drift, token-drift, missing-elements
audit-pixel-diff: pixel-minor-divergence vs pixel-systemic-divergence (env thresholds PIXEL_DIFF_THRESHOLD_MINOR, _SYSTEMIC, PIXELMATCH_THRESHOLD)
Unit test: synthetic mockup+built pair triggers each divergence class
rows.approach: Tier 4 is two-pronged: structural (DOM-level) + pixel (image-level). Structural is fast + clear; pixel catches sub-DOM visual drift that structural can't see.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Pixel diff is noisy on antialiasing + fonts; structural diff is the load-bearing signal.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/perceptual-review.ts — Tier 4 vision-LLM dispatcher. Per-screen invocation of perceptual-reviewer agent (phase0-step-040) with (mockup PNG, live PNG). Cascade-skips on Tier 3 systemic, shell-stripping, or dev-server-not-responding.
steps: Per-screen dispatcher: writes docs/build-to-spec/perceptual/<screenId>.json with structured findings
Cascade-skip flags: systemic found in Tier 3, parity found shell-stripping, dev-server unreachable
rows.approach: Perceptual judgment costs $0.029 per screen; skipping on upstream-systemic saves multiple dollars per run.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Systemic-divergence findings are downstream-irrelevant until the systemic bug is fixed; running perceptual produces noisy + redundant findings.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/walkthrough-review.ts — Tier 5 behavioral dispatcher. Single agent call with evidence bundle (Playwright walkthrough screenshots + network log + console log). Cascade-skips on Tier 3 script failure, zero screenshots, or tier disabled.
steps: Single agent dispatch with full evidence bundle; writes docs/build-to-spec/walkthrough/review.json
rows.approach: Tier 5 is once-per-fix-loop (not per-screen) — broader behavioral context across the whole flow set. Single dispatch keeps the agent's context coherent.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Loses cross-flow patterns (e.g. nav inconsistency across 3 flows); single dispatch catches them.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/bug-fix-context.ts — pre-loaded dispatch context per bug.source. Resolves fix-site files, synthesized spec, manifest data. Emits markdown block for agent prompt. Walks apps/web/app/**/page.tsx for data-screen-id matching (bug-151).
steps: Per-bug resolution: parity → fix-site = parity.screen.componentPath; synth-flows → fix-site = flow.spec path
Fallback: legacy slug-identity heuristic when data-screen-id missing
Unit test: synthetic bugs of each source produce expected fix-site
rows.approach: Pre-loaded context cuts bug-fixer's exploratory turns. Saves $0.20-$0.40 per bug-fix dispatch.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Let bug-fixer explore from BugEntry alone
rejected_alternatives.reason: Exploratory turns burn budget; context pre-load gets bug-fixer to the fix-site immediately.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/tester-diff-audit.ts — scans test-spec diff for 6 anti-patterns (seed-data-shape, url-substitution, assertion-loosening, removed-assertions, long-sleep, type-coercion-fixture, brief-scoped-out enrichment). Blocks dispatch when patterns match + no genuineProductBugs[] flag.
steps: Implement 6 line-by-line regex scanners over `git diff` output
Wire from invoke-agent post-tester (phase2-step-003 stub now becomes real)
Block path: if any pattern matches AND tester output omits genuineProductBugs[] override, fail dispatch
Unit test: each pattern detected on synthetic diff
rows.approach: Catches tester 'cheating' (loosening assertions to pass instead of fixing the bug). 6 patterns cover the empirical anti-pattern catalog from Phase 2 LESSONS.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Tester optimization pressure (pass coverage gate) creates the very anti-pattern incentive the audit catches.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/cluster-bugs.ts — pre-dispatch grouping by (source, parity.pattern, parity.screen). Folds groups ≥ threshold into systemic-divergence parent; threads cluster-parent + status into members. Per-pattern threshold overrides.
steps: Default threshold = 3 same-pattern bugs → cluster; per-pattern overrides via .claude/state/cluster-thresholds.json
Output: mutates bugs.yaml to add cluster_parent_id + cluster_member_ids
Unit test: 3 same-pattern bugs cluster; 2 don't
rows.approach: Clustering routes shared root-cause to systemic-fixer in one dispatch instead of 3 redundant bug-fixer dispatches. Saves cost + produces better fix (root vs. symptom).

rejected_alternatives.reason: Some patterns (e.g. css-pipeline-broken) cluster at 2; others (e.g. assertion-loosening) at 5. Per-pattern overrides match empirical clustering.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/verify-worktree.ts — dedicated .claude/worktrees/verify/ on detached HEAD on fix/bugs-yaml-iter branch. ensureVerifyWorktree() lazy create + reset --hard per iteration. Read-only from loop perspective; writes (bugs.yaml + plans) go to operator-facing projectRoot.
steps: Lazy create on first call; reset --hard <main-sha> per iteration
Worktree separation: bug-fixer/systemic-fixer write to verify worktree; bugs.yaml + plans live in operator-facing repo
Unit test: 3-iteration loop resets correctly + isolates operator-facing files
rows.approach: Bug-090 hardening. Without dedicated worktree, fix iterations pollute operator-facing tree with WIP commits.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Pollutes git log with WIP; operator can't review final fix cleanly.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/fix-bugs-loop.ts — automated bug-fix loop. Reads bugs.yaml; clusters via cluster-bugs (phase3-step-010); dispatches bug-fixer / systemic-fixer per-bug; verifies post-iteration via build-to-spec-verify (phase3-step-001); merges fixup branch; tracks iteration state. Cap default 10. Protected-files guard. Worktree seeding + per-bug teardown.
steps: Port Phase 2 fix-bugs-loop.ts; finish [UNFINISHED] clustering integration + final merge cascade
Per-iteration: load bugs.yaml → cluster → dispatch in parallel (cap concurrency) → re-verify → mark fixed / failed
Protected-files guard: post-dispatch rollback on violation (phase0-step-024)
Per-bug teardown: cleanup worktree state between bugs (bug-061)
Smoke against test-app with synthetic 3-bug bugs.yaml
rows.approach: Core Phase 3 driver. Cap=10 catches runaway loops; protected-files guard prevents fix-loop self-destruction.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Fix loops typically need 3-5 iterations; manual driving wastes operator time. Automation with caps is the right tradeoff.

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/{build-to-spec-verify,fix-bugs}/SKILL.md — operator entry points. build-to-spec-verify runs the 5-tier verifier; fix-bugs runs fix-bugs-loop standalone (manual ops on operator-edited bugs.yaml or retry of paused loop). Flags: --max-iterations, --max-concurrent, --dry-run.
steps: fix-bugs: invokes fix-bugs-loop with operator flag set
rows.approach: Operator-facing skill shims for the 2 main Phase 3 operations.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Verify + fix often run independently from build; separate skills match operator workflow.

rows.description: Author orchestrator/src/{round-state.ts,rounds-orchestrator.ts} — outer round loop (1→4 + final-gate round 5) wrapping fix-bugs-loop. round-state.deriveRoundState() filters pending bugs; bugsInRound() filters by round class. Terminates on clean round 5 OR outer-cap (default 8) OR no-progress.
steps: Round 1-4: bugs of specific class (e.g. round 1 = systemic; round 2 = parity; round 3 = perceptual; round 4 = walkthrough)
Round 5: final-gate — runs full verifier once more, requires zero P0/P1
Smoke against test-app with synthetic multi-class bugs.yaml
rows.approach: Round-class ordering reflects fix cost: systemic first (one fix, many bugs cleared), then per-screen perceptual, then walkthrough behavioral. Final gate validates clean state before exit.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Single round dispatches all bugs simultaneously; systemic-fix-then-re-verify is ~3× more efficient.

rows.description: Wire rounds-orchestrator into cli-runner (phase0-step-023). After Mode B completes (or on /fix-bugs invocation), run rounds-orchestrator. Persist round-state to .claude/state/{runId}/rounds.json for resume. Pause/resume integration.
steps: Extend cli-runner to invoke rounds-orchestrator post-Mode-B
Wire pause/resume: rounds-orchestrator checkpoints state per round
Smoke end-to-end: design → build → verify → 8-round fix loop on test-app
rows.approach: Closes the full pipeline: A → B → 3 → done. Each phase persists state so resume works at any boundary.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Manually invoke rounds after Mode B
rejected_alternatives.reason: Forgotten step; auto-invocation eliminates the operator gap.

rows.description: HUMAN: inspect Tier 1 build-sanity output on test-app. Confirm pnpm build + lint + typecheck pass cleanly. Catch toolchain misconfigs before downstream tiers run.
steps: If any feature fails: file plan-bug + halt verifier; debug toolchain
If all pass: drop tier-1-approved.txt with 'proceed'
rows.approach: Build-sanity failures are usually toolchain bugs (deps, configs) not feature bugs. Catching them before Tier 2-5 saves $5-$30 per run.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Auto-cascade to Tier 2 on Tier 1 fail
rejected_alternatives.reason: Tier 2-5 are vacuous when build doesn't compile; pausing for human triage is faster.

rows.description: HUMAN: inspect Tier 3 synth-flows output (Playwright walks + screenshots + console). Confirm flows complete OR file structured bugs for failures. Sign off Tier 3.
steps: Run /build-to-spec-verify --tier=3 (or full verifier; Tier 1-2 should auto-pass after row 016)
For failed flows: confirm verifier auto-filed bugs.yaml entries are accurate; add operator hints if needed
rows.approach: Tier 3 failures are usually genuine product bugs (vs Tier 1 toolchain). Operator review at this gate catches mis-classified bugs before fix-loop runs.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Tier 4-5 vision dispatches on broken flows are wasted spend; operator triage filters.

rows.description: HUMAN: inspect Tier 4 parity output (structural + pixel divergences). Calibrate operator threshold (operator may accept token-drift on first run; tighten on second). Approve.
steps: Walk divergences[]: layout-regrouping, shell-stripping, variant-drift, token-drift
Accept-as-design OR file bug per divergence (operator judgment call)
rows.approach: Parity is judgment-heavy: not every divergence is a bug (operator may have intentionally tweaked spacing). Manual gate lets operator calibrate.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Forces redoing intentional design tweaks; over-rigid parity slows iteration.

rows.description: HUMAN: inspect Tier 5 vision findings (perceptual + walkthrough). Vision-LLM hallucinations happen; operator filters before fix-loop wastes budget. Approve.
steps: Filter false positives (e.g. 'hierarchy drift' that's intentional)
Mark accepted findings as auto-fileable; mark rejected ones with operator note
Drop tier-5-approved.txt; trigger bug filing for accepted findings only
rows.approach: Vision-LLM judgments have ~10-15% false-positive rate per Phase 2 LESSONS; human filter cuts fix-loop spend by the same proportion.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Wastes ~$3 per false-positive bug-fix dispatch; filter is high-leverage.

rows.description: HUMAN: review docs/bugs.yaml after all 5 tiers complete + operator triage. Confirm bug entries are well-formed, prioritized, and accurately reflect divergences. Approve fix-loop start.
steps: Spot-check 3 random bugs against their source evidence
Add missing context (reproduction steps, hypotheses) to underspecified bugs
Approve via dropping fix-loop-approved.txt with 'proceed' OR pause + investigate further
rows.approach: bugs.yaml is the input to fix-loop; well-formed entries reduce bug-fixer exploratory turns. Operator review here pays back 3-5× in fix-loop efficiency.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Underspecified bugs cause bug-fixer to make wrong fixes that pass narrow assertions but break neighbors.

rows.description: HUMAN: confirm fix-loop convergence after rounds-orchestrator completes. Walk fixed bugs, residual bugs, accepted-known-issues. Sign off Phase 3 closure; git tag phase-3-done; mark factory ready.
steps: Read final docs/bugs.yaml + docs/build-to-spec/summary.json after rounds-orchestrator exits
Confirm clean round 5 OR document residual bugs as accepted-known-issues
Run full smoke: design → build → verify → fix on a fresh test-app to confirm reproducibility
git tag phase-3-done; update PROGRESS.md with closure note
rows.approach: Phase 3 closure gate. Fresh test-app smoke verifies the rebuild is reproducible (not just lucky on the specific test-app used during build).

rejected_alternatives.reason: Reproducibility on a new project is the real factory acceptance test; same-project closure doesn't catch project-specific state.

expected_outcomes: Fresh test-app reaches close-feature without operator hacks

rows.description: RESEARCH adopt: enable cross-worktree prompt-cache reuse. Set excludeDynamicSections:true on every query() call site; default auth-provider.ts to API-key mode for orchestrator; set ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1. Highest single-ROI change per RESEARCH §A.1, §F.6.
steps: Pin @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk to a version with excludeDynamicSections support (≥0.2.98 TS / ≥0.1.58 Python)
Patch every query() call in orchestrator/src/ to pass { excludeDynamicSections: true }
Update auth-provider.ts (phase0-step-017): default provider = anthropic-api-key for orchestrator path; Max OAuth opt-in only with explicit flag
Set ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1 in the orchestrator's process env (documented in CLAUDE.md)
Add cache-hit-ratio metric to budget-tracker (phase0-step-019): log cache_read_input_tokens / total_input_tokens per dispatch
Benchmark: Mode B 4-worktree run; worktrees 2-4 first-turn cache_read_input_tokens > 0 (currently 0 in Phase 2)
rows.approach: ProjectDiscovery published precedent: 7% → 74% → 84% cache hit rate from this single flag. With 4 worktrees × ~10K prefix tokens, this saves 30K tokens of cache writes per Mode B run (~$0.09/run on Sonnet, multiplied across N runs/day).

rejected_alternatives.approach: Keep Max OAuth default (current Phase 2 behavior)
rejected_alternatives.reason: Max OAuth is explicit ToS violation for headless SDK use as of Feb 2026; cache_control returns HTTP 400 on OAuth path as of Mar 2026. Non-starter for cache-control work.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Defer until first observed cache miss
rejected_alternatives.reason: Already observed in Phase 2; rebuild's correctness includes proving the cache-hit invariant from day one.

expected_outcomes: excludeDynamicSections set on all query() sites

rows.description: RESEARCH adopt: hook regression suite. Adversarial + benign fixture sets for block-dangerous, enforce-boundaries, protected-files, validate-brief, detect-loop. Per-PR merge gate runs the suite; >1 false-pass blocks merge. Per RESEARCH §6.
steps: Adversarial cases: rm -rf via env-var indirection, force-push via lease-then-force, protected-file rename, brief frontmatter type-coercion, identical-action with whitespace difference
Benign cases: legitimate rm in temp dir, force-with-lease, edit of non-protected file in protected dir, brief edit with new optional field, similar-but-distinct Bash calls
Author scripts/run-hook-regression.mjs: walks fixtures, runs each hook with the fixture input, asserts expected verdict
Wire as a pnpm script + recommend in .github/workflows/ (operator-installable)
rows.approach: Hook bugs are the highest-impact silent failures (a broken protected-files hook = silent rollback bypass). Regression suite catches changes to hook scripts that weaken the safety surface.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Per-hook self-tests cover happy paths; adversarial regression catches the surprising bypasses (e.g. env-var indirection) that emerge over hook iteration.

expected_outcomes: All 5 hooks have adversarial + benign fixtures
rows.polished_waiver_reason: Hook regression suite: 24 fixtures, <2s wall-clock. No meaningful budget to verify.

rows.description: RESEARCH adopt: SSIM Tier 4.5 perceptual prefilter. New orchestrator/src/perceptual-prefilter.ts using sharp-phash + ssim.js + odiff. Routes screens labeled 'identical' past Tiers 4-5 entirely; 'near' to Haiku 4.5; 'diff' to Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.7. Per RESEARCH §3.3, §5.
steps: Author packages/perceptual-cascade/ with sharp-phash + ssim.js + odiff deps

Author orchestrator/src/perceptual-prefilter.ts: classify(mockupPng, livePng) → 'identical' | 'near' | 'diff' (thresholds: pHash Hamming ≤1 AND mSSIM ≥0.998 → identical; Hamming ≤4 AND mSSIM ≥0.99 → near; else diff)
Add content-addressed screenshot cache keyed on (git tree hash + dev-server build digest) — skip rerender on unchanged trees
Ship in shadow mode first: log prefilter verdict + 'would-have-saved' token estimate; do not skip until validated
Benchmark: prefilter false-positive rate (declared identical, broken) ≤1% on 200-screen corpus before enabling cascade-skip
Flip passes:true (initial pass = shadow mode; cascade-skip enable is follow-up polish row)
rows.approach: Per RESEARCH §5: for medium 25-screen app on fix-loop iteration 2+, this cuts perceptual+walkthrough vision calls by ~70-85%. Cost: $4-$10/day saved on parity alone, plus narrower Opus blast radius.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Discriminators catch systemic misconfigs (Tailwind broken, etc.); they don't catch per-screen visual identity. SSIM is the cheap-per-screen filter.
ssim.js + sharp-phash are TS-native; no Python toolchain dependency; ~25% absolute SSIM divergence from scikit-image is acceptable for the binary threshold use case.

expected_outcomes: Prefilter classifies into 3 buckets correctly on synthetic mockup/live pairs
Shadow-mode logging shows ≥50% identical on iteration-2+ runs

rows.description: RESEARCH adopt: Haiku 4.5 routing for routine vision + cluster augmentation. perceptual-reviewer + walkthrough-reviewer default Haiku 4.5; escalate to Sonnet 4.6 only when SSIM dissimilarity >1% (per phase3-step-022). cluster-bugs gets Haiku-as-second-pass clusterer (~$0.0135/round). Per RESEARCH §3.2, §3.5.
steps: Update .claude/models.yaml (phase0-step-018): perceptual-reviewer default = haiku-4-5; walkthrough-reviewer default = haiku-4-5
Add per-agent-mode override: perceptual-reviewer.modes.escalated = sonnet-4-6 (triggered by prefilter verdict='diff')

Extend cluster-bugs.ts (phase3-step-010): post-heuristic Haiku 4.5 pass; dispatch systemic-fixer only when both agree on cluster size ≥3 (defense in depth — false-merge wastes $2-5, false-split wastes N small calls)
Cost guard in budget-tracker: log per-model dispatch ratio; alert if Sonnet ratio on perceptual > 30% (calibration drift)
Smoke against test-app: verify routing + escalation work correctly
rows.approach: Haiku 4.5 has full vision support at 1/3 cost + 2× speed of Sonnet 4. Routine parity passes don't need Sonnet judgment; SSIM escalation catches the cases that do. Cluster-bugs Haiku audit is defense-in-depth at trivial cost.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Pure cost overhead on routine identical/near-identical screens. RESEARCH benchmarks: ~70%+ shift to Haiku on parity passes after this change.
Haiku 4.5 vision is not deeply benchmarked for dense data-viz or 8pt-font tables (per RESEARCH caveats); Sonnet escalation is the safety net.

expected_outcomes: Default routing to Haiku for both vision agents

rows.description: Author factory-root scaffolds missed in initial pass: brief-template.md (canonical 20-section template with frontmatter — /draft-brief copies into every new project), mcp-defaults-design.json (design-scope MCP list with scoped_to per agent), factory tsconfig.base.json + factory pnpm-workspace.yaml + factory package.json.

Port mcp-defaults-design.json with all design-stage servers (playwright, icons8, unsplash, chrome-devtools, image-generator) + scoped_to + config blocks

Verify `just --list` from .claude project dir lists all recipes

rows.description: Author scripts/ schema validators paired with schemas/ (phase0-step-030): validate-architecture.mjs, validate-brief.mjs, validate-feature-context.mjs, validate-screens.mjs, validate-tasks-yaml.mjs. Each loads its JSON schema + AJV-validates a file at a CLI-supplied path.

Each: shebang + commander/argv parse + ajv + structured exit codes (0 = valid, 1 = invalid, 2 = file/schema not found)
Each prints validation errors with file:line context
Smoke each on a known-good + known-bad fixture

rejected_alternatives.reason: Operator + CI need CLI-validate; CLI scripts work outside the TS toolchain (e.g. pre-commit hook, GitHub Action).

rows.description: Author scripts/ factory helpers (~15 scripts): build-screens-catalog.mjs, build-screens-manifest.mjs, build-user-flows.mjs, derive-fixture-from-mockup.mjs, retrofit-ui-kit-data-attrs.mjs, file-bug-plan.mjs, snapshot-project.mjs, sync-project-schemas.mjs, aggregate-components.mjs, ai-walkthrough.mjs, run-synthesized-flows.mjs, seed-app-state.mjs, archive-plans.mjs, detect-affects-files-overlaps.mjs, audit-tracked-pycache.mjs.
steps: Port each script verbatim from Phase 2 scripts/
Each: shebang + commander/argv parse + clear stdin/stdout contract

Cross-wire: file-bug-plan.mjs invoked by build-to-spec-verify auto-file (phase3-step-001)
Smoke each against test-app where applicable

rejected_alternatives.approach: Author each helper in its own row

rows.description: POST-MVP adopt: cost-projection-preview. Extends budget-tracker (phase0-step-019) with pre-dispatch cost forecast — given an agent + prompt size + expected output, estimate spend before query() fires. Operator can abort high-cost dispatches before they incur.
steps: Author orchestrator/src/cost-projection.ts: forecast(agentSlug, inputTokens, expectedOutputTokens) → { sonnet: $, opus: $, haiku: $ } using model-config (phase0-step-018) tier lookup
Wire forecast into invoke-agent (phase0-step-022) pre-dispatch: if forecast > per-stage-cap × 0.5, log warning; if > per-stage-cap, throw BudgetExceededError
Add /preview-cost operator skill: takes a planned stage + projected inputs; reports forecast without dispatching
Smoke: forecast() matches actual spend within ±15% on 5 known dispatches
rows.approach: Adopted from post-mvp-scaffolding/cost-projection-preview.md. Pairs cleanly with budget-tracker; pre-dispatch forecast is small extension over post-dispatch recording.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Forecast only after spend accumulates patterns
rejected_alternatives.reason: Operator wants forecast BEFORE costly dispatches (mockups $5-10, systemic-fixer $1-4). Reactive accumulation is too late.

expected_outcomes: Forecast within ±15% of actuals on smoke set
rows.polished_waiver_reason: Pure-function cost-projection: <1ms per forecast. No meaningful budget to verify.

rows.description: Author .claude/skills/resume-build/SKILL.md — operator skill paired with pause-build (phase2-step-013). Resumes a paused Mode B run from .claude/state/{pipelineRunId}/feature-graph.json; respects pause-granularity (feature-boundary vs mid-feature).
steps: Skill body: read .claude/state/{runId}/feature-graph.json + paused.json; invoke runFeatureGraph with --resume-feature-graph flag
If pause-granularity = mid-feature, resume from exact agent boundary; if feature-boundary, resume at next feature in topo order
Flag: --pipeline-run-id <id> (operator selects which paused run to resume; --latest auto-selects most recent)
Smoke: pause → resume round-trip on 3-feature graph
rows.approach: Resume is the operator complement to pause-build. Without it, pause is a one-way trip that wastes Mode B state.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Operator may want to inspect paused state before resuming; auto-resume hides the gap.

rows.description: POST-MVP adopt: partial-failure-policy. Extends feature-graph (phase2-step-002) with per-feature recovery semantics. On feature failure: mark failed in tasks.yaml, continue dependent-free siblings, skip downstream blocked features, emit failure summary at graph end.
steps: Extend feature-graph.ts: on per-feature exhaust, mark task status=failed (not abort whole graph)
Compute reachable-failure-blast-radius: which downstream features become un-runnable
Continue with sibling features whose deps are still satisfied
Emit docs/partial-failure-report.md at graph end with failed[], blocked[], succeeded[] tables
Flag: --abort-on-failure (operator opts back to all-or-nothing if preferred)
Smoke: synthetic 5-feature graph with feature-2 failure; verify features 3+5 still run (independent), feature-4 marked blocked (depends on 2)
rows.approach: Adopted from post-mvp-scaffolding/partial-failure-policy.md. Pairs with feature-graph's pending [UNFINISHED] fine-grained pause — same code paths, different exit conditions.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Wastes 4 successful features when 1 fails; partial completion is often acceptable for non-blocking features.

expected_outcomes: Synthetic 5-feature graph behaves per blast-radius rule

rows.description: POST-MVP adopt: runtime-signoff-gate. Adds Gate 7 (post-fix-loop, pre-deploy). After rounds-orchestrator clean exit, operator runtime-tests the built app + signs off OR files final-issue plan. Prevents deploy of apps that pass tier verification but fail real-world use.

rows.description: Author factory-root developer-experience config: .markdownlint.jsonc + .markdownlint-cli2.jsonc (MD043 lock on brief 20-section heading list + MD041 first-line H1 + MD025 only-one-H1), .gitignore, .prettierignore, .github/workflows/validate-brief.yml (CI gate enforcing brief structure on PRs touching brief.md).

rows.approach: Tests are part of the rebuild's correctness story. Per-row 'unit test' steps in module rows are aspirational; this row materializes the actual Phase 2 test surface as one batch. Bootstrapping the workspace first lets every module row's self-test entry actually execute.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Author tests row-by-row paired with each module
rejected_alternatives.reason: Test code is already authored in Phase 2; per-row authoring would duplicate the port effort + risk inconsistent vitest config across files.

rejected_alternatives.approach: Skip test port; let new tests grow organically
rejected_alternatives.reason: Phase 2's tests encode the empirical bugs caught during 200+ bug history; losing them re-opens the same surface.
